title_en: Al-Dhahabi's Critical Method: Weighing Praise and Blame
title_ar: ميزان الاعتدال في نقد الرجال — الجزء الثاني
content_en:
Al-Dhahabi's critical method in Mizan al-I'tidal is distinctive for its combination of encyclopedic knowledge, genuine independent judgment, and a concern for fairness that sometimes leads him to defend narrators against unjust criticism. Understanding his method requires examining how he approaches the conflicting assessments that the earlier tradition had accumulated for disputed narrators.

The Problem of Conflicting Assessments

Many narrators in the hadith literature received contradictory assessments from different earlier scholars. One critic might describe a narrator as weak while another considers him acceptable; one scholar might accuse him of a specific error while another defends the contested transmission. Al-Dhahabi's task in these cases is to weigh the competing assessments, consider the relative authority and knowledge of the critics, and arrive at the most defensible overall judgment.

His general principle follows the classical rule that disparagement (jarh) takes precedence over accreditation (tadil) when the grounds for criticism are specified and the critic has relevant knowledge. But al-Dhahabi applies this principle with nuance: he notes when critics may have had personal motives, when criticism was based on a single disputed transmission rather than a pattern of unreliability, and when the overall body of evidence strongly favors the narrator despite isolated criticism.

Categories of Weak Narrators

Al-Dhahabi develops a nuanced vocabulary for classifying weak narrators. He distinguishes between: narrators who are simply imprecise (layyin or fihi layna), whose hadiths may be cited with caution; those who are weak (da'if) and whose hadiths require strong corroboration; those who are severely weak (da'if jiddan) and whose hadiths should generally be avoided; those who are abandoned (matruk) due to consistent unreliability; and those who are accused of fabrication (muttaham bi al-wadc) and whose hadiths must be discarded entirely.

In practice, his assessments are more granular than these categories suggest. He will often note that a narrator is weak in hadiths from a particular teacher but acceptable when reporting from others; that a narrator's early reports are reliable but his later ones (often indicating memory deterioration in old age) should be treated with caution; or that a narrator is reliable in one legal subject area but weak in another where he had less expertise.

Al-Dhahabi's Defense of Unjustly Criticized Narrators

One of the most valuable aspects of Mizan al-I'tidal is al-Dhahabi's willingness to defend narrators whom he considers unjustly or excessively criticized. He occasionally notes that a critic's condemnation of a narrator was motivated by theological disagreement rather than evidence of actual unreliability in transmission — for example, when Sunni critics condemned narrators affiliated with Mutazilite or Shia tendencies without establishing that their theological views affected their accuracy in transmitting hadith.

Al-Dhahabi was himself a theologian of the Athari school with strong views on proper Sunni belief, but he maintained a distinction between theological unreliability and hadith unreliability. A narrator who held heterodox theological views was not automatically disqualified from transmitting hadith if his transmissions were actually accurate and his moral character otherwise sound. This methodological sophistication makes al-Dhahabi a more reliable guide to narrator quality than critics who conflated theological preference with hadith evaluation.
title_id: Metode Kritis Al-Dzahabi: Menimbang Pujian dan Celaan
content_id:
Metode kritis al-Dzahabi dalam Mizan al-I'tidal khas karena kombinasinya antara pengetahuan yang ensiklopedis, penilaian mandiri yang sejati, dan kepedulian terhadap keadilan yang kadang-kadang membawanya untuk membela perawi-perawi yang para kritikus sebelumnya perlakukan terlalu keras. Ketiga kualitas ini bersama-sama membedakannya dari kritikus yang sekedar mengkompilasi penilaian-penilaian orang lain tanpa kontribusi analitis sendiri.

Pendekatan al-Dzahabi terhadap perawi yang menerima penilaian yang beragam — dipuji oleh beberapa kritikus dan dicela oleh yang lain — mencontohkan pendekatannya. Alih-alih hanya mendaftar penilaian-penilaian tanpa berusaha mensintesiskannya, al-Dzahabi sering menyatakan pandangannya sendiri tentang penilaian mana yang ia temukan paling meyakinkan dan mengapa. Penilaian-penilaian independen ini sangat berharga karena mencerminkan keputusan dari seorang yang sepenuhnya menguasai literatur dan yang memiliki kemampuan untuk menilai bobot relatif dari sumber-sumber yang saling bertentangan.

Kepedulian al-Dzahabi terhadap keadilan terlihat jelas dalam cara beliau menangani perawi-perawi yang dikritik atas dasar keyakinan mereka daripada atas dasar keandalan transmisi mereka. Beliau membedakan dengan cermat antara perawi yang lemah dalam hafalan atau akurasi dan perawi yang pandangan teologisnya tidak ortodoks tetapi yang transmisi mereka sebenarnya dapat diandalkan. Perbedaan ini penting: perawi dengan keyakinan yang menyimpang mungkin masih menghasilkan transmisi yang akurat, sementara perawi dengan keyakinan yang ortodoks mungkin memiliki hafalan yang lemah. Al-Dzahabi menangani kedua jenis permasalahan tersebut secara eksplisit.
content_ar:
في كتاب "ميزان الاعتدال في نقد الرجال" للحافظ الذهبي، يستعرض الذهبي في هذا الجزء تراجم عدد من رواة الحديث مع بيان أحوالهم توثيقًا وتجريحًا مما له صلة بالموضوع المعنون أعلاه، مستعينًا بالأدلة الشرعية من الكتاب والسنة وأقوال الأئمة والعلماء.

ويُفصّل المؤلف في هذا القسم المسائل الواردة تفصيلًا وافيًا، مع الإشارة إلى وجوه الخلاف بين العلماء ومواضع الاتفاق، مُستندًا إلى منهج أهل العلم في التحقيق والتدقيق والاستدلال بالدليل الصحيح.

ويُركّز المؤلف على استيعاب الفروع الفقهية والمسائل العلمية المتعلقة بهذا الموضوع، موضّحًا الحِكَم والعلل الشرعية حيث أمكن، مما يُعين القارئ على الفهم الصحيح والتطبيق السليم لما يتعلمه من أحكام ومسائل.

ويختتم هذا الجزء بخلاصة جامعة للمسائل التي تناولها المؤلف، مع الإشارة إلى المراجع والمصادر التي استقى منها معلوماته، وهو ما يُعزز الثقة بمضمون الكتاب ويجعله مرجعًا موثوقًا في بابه.
